step_by_step_thinking:
Amalgam is characterized primarily by its ductility, meaning it can deform plastically under pressure before fracturing. Beveling the occlusal margin of an amalgam restoration could compromise the structural integrity since even small cracks can propagate through the flexible nature of the material. Therefore, it is generally undesirable to bevel the occlusal margins of an amalgam cavity preparation.
\boxed{B} Based on the properties of amalgam highlighted in the articles—specifically its ductility—it is clear that beveling the occlusal margins of an amalgam filling could lead to weaknesses in the restoration’s structure, allowing propagation of minor defects.
